channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Childhood bullying survivor reflects on lifelong impact and decision not to have children
item.description: The article is a first-person account of severe bullying experienced by the writer from the age of five after moving to a new village school. Early incidents included physical and sexualised bullying ...

item.title: Nakuru ECDE teacher reported missing after leaving home for first day at work
item.description: A 31-year-old Early Childhood Development Education (ECDE) teacher, Mercy Nyaboke, has been reported missing after she disappeared while heading to her new job in Nakuru County. According to her famil...

item.title: BJP wins Falta repoll in West Bengal, strengthening position in state assembly
item.description: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) secured a significant victory in the Falta assembly constituency repoll in West Bengal, further consolidating its political position in the state. The Election Commiss...

item.title: More than 1,000 Alberta students take part in APEGA Science Olympics in Calgary
item.description: More than 1,000 students gathered in Calgary to participate in the APEGA Science Olympics, a large-scale educational event focused on science, engineering, and hands-on learning challenges. The compet...

item.title: Abdeslam Ouaddou to review Orlando Pirates future after leading club to domestic treble
item.description: Orlando Pirates coach Abdeslam Ouaddou says he needs time to rest and reflect before deciding whether he will remain at the club next season after guiding the Buccaneers to a historic domestic treble....

item.title: Archival Photographs of Metallica’s Early Years at Their El Cerrito Headquarters (1983–1986)
item.description: The article from Vintage Everyday presents a curated set of candid photographs documenting Metallica during a crucial formative period between 1983 and 1986 at their informal headquarters known as the...

item.title: Klarisse De Guzman joins ‘The ChooSing One’ segment on Everybody Sing
item.description: The ABS-CBN Studios article published on May 24, 2026 highlights singer Klarisse De Guzman’s appearance in the popular musical game show ‘Everybody Sing’. In the feature, Klarisse is noted for bringin...

item.title: Heatwaves reduce milk production and increase losses for dairy farmers in Uttar Pradesh
item.description: Dairy farmers across Uttar Pradesh are facing increasing financial and livestock-related challenges as rising summer temperatures and heatwaves affect cattle health and milk production. The article hi...

item.title: Democratic Lawmaker Forecasts Growing Republican Distance From Trump Ahead of Midterms
item.description: A senior House Democrat has predicted that internal divisions within the Republican Party will deepen in the coming months, particularly as lawmakers return to their districts during congressional rec...

item.title: Young female baseball players gather in Calgary to develop skills and promote girls’ participation in the sport
item.description: A baseball development event focused on young female athletes took place at the big diamond in Confederation Park in Calgary, giving participants the opportunity to improve their baseball abilities wh...

item.title: Free Software Foundation reports 46 LibreLocal meetups across six continents, with more events planned
item.description: The Free Software Foundation (FSF) announced that its global initiative encouraging community meetups, called LibreLocals, has seen strong international participation. As part of LibreLocal month in M...

item.title: Study suggests banana-containing smoothies may reduce absorption of beneficial flavanols
item.description: A study from the University of California, Davis suggests that common smoothie combinations may significantly influence how the body absorbs beneficial plant compounds. Researchers found that adding b...

item.title: Victorian default electricity prices to fall for households and small businesses in 2026-27
item.description: More than 500,000 Victorian households and around 62,000 small businesses are expected to benefit from lower electricity prices in the next financial year after the Essential Services Commission (ESC)...

item.title: Experts Advise Grandparents to Avoid Certain Common Phrases That May Harm Children’s Emotional Well-Being
item.description: The article explores how well-intentioned comments from grandparents can sometimes negatively affect their grandchildren’s emotional development, self-esteem, and sense of safety. According to pediatr...

item.title: African countries with no outstanding IMF debt as of May 2026 and what it means for their economies
item.description: The article examines the status of African countries that have no outstanding loans with the International Monetary Fund (IMF) as of May 2026. It highlights that while many African economies continue ...

item.title: Evosha Energies opens new fuel station in Mbezi to improve fuel access for residents and transport operators
item.description: Evosha Energies has officially launched a new fuel station at Mbezi Makabe junction in Dar es Salaam, aiming to improve access to petroleum products for residents, motorists, and bodaboda riders in on...

item.title: Gauteng records sharp rise in informal settlements and land invasions
item.description: Gauteng has recorded a major increase in informal settlements, with provincial data showing that there are now more than 860,000 informal structures spread across 1,158 settlements. The figures were p...

item.title: The Tradition of “Penny for the Guy” in Mid-20th Century Britain and Its Decline
item.description: “Penny for the Guy” was once a central part of British childhood culture, especially during the mid-20th century around Bonfire Night on November 5th. Before Halloween traditions like trick-or-treatin...

item.title: Analysis of how $5.5 million in donations were distributed following the Lapu-Lapu Day attack in Vancouver
item.description: Following the deadly Lapu-Lapu Day Festival attack in Vancouver that killed 11 people and injured dozens, more than $5.5 million was raised through charities and online fundraising campaigns to suppor...

item.title: Rape survivor questions court process after teenage offenders spared jail
item.description: A teenage girl who was raped by two teenage boys has spoken to the BBC about her distress following their sentencing, after the offenders were spared prison. The victim, who was 15 at the time of the ...
